ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Singaporean economic rice (cai fan). The closest defensible parent class is 'cooked rice dish' (FOODON_03315595). A more specific term is not available in the current FoodOn release.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated code for Singaporean economic rice. A00LC (rice-based composite dish) is the closest defensible category anchor. Exact match not available.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific Singaporean hawker dish. SNOMED CT coverage of regional composite rice dishes is limited.

main_ingredients: mixed vegetables (e.g. stir-fried leafy greens, braised tofu, egg)
mixed meats (e.g. braised pork, steamed chicken, fish cake)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard hawker plate of economic rice with two vegetable dishes and two meat dishes weighs approximately 400 g, comprising roughly 200 g of steamed white rice and 200 g of mixed toppings.

nutrient_source.title: Economic Rice (2 Vegetables, 2 Meats) — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Economic Rice (2 Vegetables, 2 Meats), cr_id F05100042. All per-100g values are taken as reported. Confidence 0.95 as assigned by HPB.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — white rice (steamed, Asian-style) GI ~72
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Singaporean economic rice (cai fan) as a composite dish in the University of Sydney GI Database. The GI value of 72 is derived from the analogous food 'steamed white rice (Asian-style)' reported in Atkinson FS et al. (2021), Diabetes Care, which is the dominant carbohydrate component of this dish. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (72 × 55.3) / 100 ≈ 40.0, based on a 400 g serving providing approximately 55.3 g available carbohydrate (13.83 g/100g × 400 g).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 248 mg/100g; low dietary fibre (1.33 g/100g) limits potassium-sodium offset
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request reduced soy and oyster sauce on all dishes, choose steamed or braised lean protein over salty preserved meats, and limit portion size to reduce total sodium intake per meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI white rice base (GI 72) with 13.83 g/100g carbohydrate and low dietary fibre (1.33 g/100g) produces a high glycaemic load per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should reduce the rice portion to half, increase the vegetable component, choose non-starchy vegetables and lean protein toppings, and avoid sweet braised or glazed meat preparations to moderate postprandial blood glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate fat content (6.79 g/100g) with unknown saturated fat fraction; sodium at 248 mg/100g contributes to cardiovascular risk if consumed frequently
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should select steamed or braised lean meats (chicken breast, fish, tofu) over fatty pork cuts, request minimal added oil in vegetable preparation, and limit this meal to occasional consumption given the moderate sodium and uncharacterised saturated fat load.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Potential arachidonic acid load from red meat selections; vegetable dishes provide antioxidants
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid red meat (pork, beef) toppings to minimise arachidonic acid intake, request minimal cooking oil in vegetable dishes, and favour fish or tofu as protein selections to support an anti-inflammatory dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Economic Rice (2 Vegetables, 2 Meats), known in Mandarin as 经济饭 (jīng jì fàn) and in Malay as Nasi Ekonomi, is a quintessential Singaporean hawker dish served at cai fan (mixed rice) stalls across the island. The dish consists of a base of steamed white jasmine rice topped with a customer-selected combination of two vegetable dishes and two meat dishes from a display of pre-cooked options. Common vegetable choices include stir-fried leafy greens (kangkong, kai lan, chye sim), braised tofu, or egg dishes; common meat choices include braised pork belly, steamed chicken, fish cake, luncheon meat, or fried egg. The dish is typically seasoned with soy sauce, oyster sauce, and cooking oil applied during preparation of the individual components. Per 100 g, the dish provides 147 kcal, 7.52 g protein, 6.79 g fat, 13.83 g carbohydrate, 1.33 g dietary fibre, and 248 mg sodium, as reported by the HPB Singapore Foodo database. The white rice base carries a high glycaemic index of approximately 72, producing a high glycaemic load per standard 400 g serving. Clinically, this dish is of moderate-to-high concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes due to the high-GI rice base and low fibre content, and of moderate concern for hypertension due to sodium from sauces. It is a culturally significant, affordable, and widely consumed lunch option for working adults in Singapore, typically priced between SGD 3.50 and SGD 5.00 per plate.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05100042) with a reported confidence of 0.95. HPB is the primary approved source for Singaporean dishes. The GI value is estimated from the analogous food 'steamed white rice (Asian-style)' from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. (2021), which is the dominant carbohydrate component. All mandatory multilingual names are present. Overall confidence is high.
notes: 1. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this composite hawker dish are not individually reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for F05100042, and no equivalent breakdown is available in USDA FoodData Central for this specific Singaporean composite dish as of 2026. 2. The GI value of 72 is an analogous estimate based on steamed white rice (Asian-style) from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al. (2021), Diabetes Care, as no direct GI measurement for economic rice as a composite dish is available in an approved GI source. Glycaemic load per serving calculated at 400 g serving size: GL = (72 × 55.32) / 100 ≈ 40.0. 3. The dietary_tags in the input ('vegetarian', 'plant_based', 'low_calorie') have been removed as they are inconsistent with a dish containing two meat components; is_vegetarian and is_vegan are correctly set to false. 4. FoodOn and FoodEx2 codes are approximate matches; no exact ontology code exists for Singaporean economic rice (cai fan) in current public releases. 5. Halal status is not confirmed as meat selection varies by stall; is_halal is set to false pending stall-level verification.
